summary: Implements MessageRepository with insert-or-ignore deduplication and date-range/recency queries over the messages table

guarantees: Create is idempotent: duplicate (channel_id, telegram_msg_id) is silently dropped, no error returned
Create mutates message.ID and message.FetchedAt in-place via RETURNING when the row is new
GetDistinctDates silently skips rows whose date string cannot be parsed

effects: SQLite reads and writes on messages table

observations.text: Create uses ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING + RETURNING. When a duplicate exists, RETURNING returns no row → sql.ErrNoRows is swallowed. The caller's message.ID stays zero — callers must not rely on ID being populated for duplicates.
GetDistinctDates extracts dates via SUBSTR(sent_at, 1, 10) rather than SQLite DATE() because Go's time driver stores timestamps as full RFC3339 strings; DATE() may not parse them correctly.
GetDistinctDates silently continues on parse errors (time.Parse failure → continue). A corrupted timestamp value in the DB produces a missing date in the result, not an error.
